dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8842 IH: Federal Agents Secure Travel Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Rutherford (for himself and Mr. Cuellar) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Homeland Security

form.official-title: To amend title 49, United States Code, to provide eligibility for Federal law enforcement officers for certain expedited security screening, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Agents Secure Travel Act or the FAST Act.

section.header: Eligibility for Federal law enforcement officers for certain expedited security screening
section.text: Section 44919 of title 49, United States Code is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsections (j), (k), and (l) as subsections (k), (l), and (m), respectively; and
by inserting after subsection (i) the following new subsection:

subsection.header: Eligibility of Federal law enforcement officers for expedited security screening

paragraph.text: Subject to paragraph (3), an individual specified in paragraph (2) is eligible for expedited security screening under the PreCheck Program at no cost to such individual.
An individual specified in this subsection is any of the following:

subparagraph.text: A family member of an individual specified in subparagraph (A) who is younger than 12 years old and accompanying the individual.

paragraph.text: The eligibility of an individual specified in paragraph (2) for expedited security screening under the PreCheck Program is subject to such policies and procedures as the Administrator may prescribe to carry out this subsection.
In this subsection, the term Federal law enforcement officer has the meaning given such term in section 2 of the Law Enforcement Congressional Badge of Bravery Act of 2008 (34 U.S.C. 50301).
